skill.name: code-conventions
skill.description: Guides AI coding agents to follow Deno-focused formatting and documentation conventions.
skill.summary: Apply Deno formatting and documentation standards consistently.
skill.author: 5t111111

security_audit.summary: Static weak cryptography findings at SKILL.md lines 3 and 8 are false positives from descriptive text. The skill does instruct agents to run `mise run fmt`, which is a legitimate formatting workflow but still executes a project-defined external command.

medium_findings.title: External Formatting Command
medium_findings.description: The skill tells agents to run `mise run fmt` before committing. This is normal for code formatting, but it can execute project-defined tasks and should only be run in trusted repositories.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command is directly present and not interpolated from user input. Risk depends on the repository's mise task configuration, so legitimate use is likely but external execution remains relevant.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Weak Cryptography Matches
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography at descriptive lines, but those lines only describe formatting guidance. No cryptographic code, algorithm choice, or security-sensitive operation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain plain-language descriptions. I found no evidence of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or other weak cryptographic algorithm usage.

dangerous_patterns.title: Project Task Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The formatter command delegates behavior to the repository's mise task definition. Users should review project tasks before allowing an AI agent to run them.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The external command is explicit and relevant to the skill's purpose. It is not inherently malicious, but task delegation creates a contextual execution risk.

content.user_title: Apply Code Conventions for Deno Projects
content.value_statement: Inconsistent formatting and missing comments make Deno code harder to review. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to format code and document symbols before commits.
actual_capabilities: Reminds agents to run `mise run fmt` before committing code.
Encourages consistent formatting for Deno-based projects.
Requires documentation comments for functions, classes, and properties.
Specifies JSDoc or TSDoc as the documentation format.
Directs agents to write documentation comments in English.
limitations: Does not define naming rules, test standards, or architecture patterns.
Does not inspect whether `mise run fmt` is available in a repository.
Does not provide automated linting or formatting scripts.
Assumes the project uses Deno or compatible tooling.

use_cases.title: Prepare Deno Changes for Review
use_cases.description: Apply formatting and documentation checks before opening a pull request.

use_cases.title: Standardize AI Code Contributions
use_cases.description: Guide AI agents to follow the same documentation expectations across generated code.

use_cases.title: Document Public TypeScript Symbols
use_cases.description: Add clear JSDoc or TSDoc comments to functions, classes, and properties.

prompt_templates.title: Format My Changes
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my current changes against the code conventions. Format the code with the project formatter and tell me what changed.

prompt_templates.title: Add Documentation Comments
prompt_templates.prompt: Find functions, classes, and properties that need JSDoc or TSDoc comments. Add concise English documentation where it is missing.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a Pull Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare this Deno project change for review. Apply formatting, check documentation comments, and summarize any remaining convention gaps.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Large Change Set
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit the staged changes for this project's code conventions. Verify formatting, English documentation comments, and symbol coverage before commit.

output_examples.input: A Deno change contains new functions without comments.
output_examples.output: The assistant adds short English TSDoc comments and reports which symbols were documented.

output_examples.input: A developer asks for pre-commit cleanup.
output_examples.output: The assistant runs the configured formatter, confirms formatting status, and notes any documentation gaps.

output_examples.input: A pull request changes classes and properties.
output_examples.output: The assistant checks that each changed symbol has JSDoc or TSDoc coverage.

best_practices: Review the repository's mise tasks before allowing command execution.
Keep documentation comments short, factual, and written in English.
Run formatting after code edits and before final review.
anti_patterns: Do not run formatter tasks in an untrusted repository without review.
Do not add placeholder comments that repeat the symbol name.
Do not use this skill as a substitute for project-specific lint rules.

faq.question: What projects does this skill fit best?
faq.answer: It fits Deno or TypeScript projects that use mise for formatting and expect JSDoc or TSDoc comments.

faq.question: Does it install formatting tools?
faq.answer: No. It only tells the agent to use the existing `mise run fmt` project command.

faq.question: Can it work with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The marketplace metadata lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it enforce security rules?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on formatting and documentation conventions, not security scanning.

faq.question: Why is there a security warning?
faq.answer: The skill recommends an external formatting command. Users should review project task definitions before running it.

faq.question: Does it require comments in English?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill instructs agents to write documentation comments in English.
